name: Israel Methcomm
description_short: Israel Methcomm Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union serving the Chicago community with NCUA insurance protection and traditional credit union services.
description_long:
Israel Methcomm Federal Credit Union is a federally chartered, member-owned credit union operating as a not-for-profit financial institution. Based on the available website information, the organization maintains its main office in Chicago, Illinois, and is registered with the National Credit Union Service Organization (NCUSO), indicating NCUA insurance coverage for member deposits. The credit union serves a specific membership base in the Chicago metropolitan area.

As a federal credit union, Israel Methcomm offers traditional credit union products and services typical of member-owned financial institutions, including share accounts (savings), share certificates, and personal asset loans. The organization operates through a physical branch network, with members able to access services at their main office location on South Cottage Grove Avenue. The credit union can be contacted directly for details about specific products, rates, and membership eligibility requirements.

What distinguishes Israel Methcomm as a credit union is its not-for-profit, member-owned structure, which means any profits are theoretically returned to members rather than shareholders. The organization holds federal credit union status and NCUA insurance protection, providing regulatory oversight and deposit security. The specific membership field of membership and any specialized lending programs are not detailed in the available public information.

Prospective members should contact the credit union directly to confirm current membership eligibility, available loan products, interest rates on savings and certificates, and any membership fees. The limited public information available online suggests conducting a direct phone consultation at (773) 483-1131 is necessary to fully understand the scope of services, lending terms, and whether membership requirements align with your situation.
services: In-person banking at main office branch
pros: NCUA-insured institution providing federal deposit insurance protection for member accounts
Not-for-profit structure means earnings are returned to members rather than external shareholders
Member-owned governance allows members to have a voice in institutional decisions
Federal credit union status provides regulatory oversight and stability
Physical branch location in Chicago accessible for in-person service and account management
Direct phone support available at (773) 483-1131 for member inquiries
Established presence in Chicago community with registered NCUSO membership
cons: Limited publicly available information about specific loan products, rates, and terms online
Membership eligibility requirements not clearly stated on public pages
No online service details or digital banking capabilities mentioned in available website content
Requires direct phone contact for basic product and service information
No information about whether institution serves broader geographic area beyond Chicago location
best_for: Chicago-area residents eligible for membership seeking NCUA-insured savings accounts
Individuals preferring member-owned, not-for-profit financial institutions over banks
Members seeking traditional credit union personal loans and share certificate products
diagnosis: Israel Methcomm Federal Credit Union is best for Chicago-area residents seeking a member-owned, NCUA-insured alternative to traditional banks with traditional credit union products. However, the main caveat is that very limited service details are publicly available; prospective members must contact the credit union directly at (773) 483-1131 to confirm membership eligibility, specific loan products, rates, and terms.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
